content: Just as hierarchical working memory chunking by subgoals improves long-horizon retention for individual active learning agents, a hierarchical subgoal-structured Agent-to-Agent communication protocol can reduce collective working memory overload and improve retention of long-horizon task state across distributed agent teams. This insight repurposes individual working memory design principles to solve a key unaddressed gap in open multi-agent coordination.
source_topics: Working memory retention mechanisms for long-horizon active learning
trigger_topic: Working memory retention mechanisms for long-horizon active learning
